description: Add a review agent that runs between DAG levels to check completed work and create fix stories if needed

userStories.title: Add review event types to events.rs
userStories.description: Add three new event variants to the BaroEvent enum in crates/baro-tui/src/events.rs: ReviewStart { level: usize }, ReviewLog { line: String }, ReviewComplete { level: usize, passed: bool, fix_count: u32 }. These must use serde rename tags matching the existing pattern (e.g. #[serde(rename = "review_start")]). Do not modify any existing variants.

userStories.title: Add review tracking state to App and handle review events
userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/app.rs: 1) Add three new fields to the App struct: review_in_progress: bool, review_level: usize, review_logs: Vec<String>. 2) Initialize them in App::new() (false, 0, empty vec). 3) Add match arms in handle_event() for the three new review events: ReviewStart sets review_in_progress=true and review_level=level and clears review_logs. ReviewLog appends line to review_logs (cap at MAX_LOG_LINES like story logs). ReviewComplete sets review_in_progress=false. Do not modify any existing event handling logic.

userStories.title: Implement review agent in executor.rs
userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/executor.rs, add a review step in run_executor() that runs after all stories in each level complete but before moving to the next level. Implementation: 1) Before executing stories in a level, save the current git commit hash using 'git rev-parse HEAD'. 2) After all stories in a level complete (after the handles loop), run the review: a) Emit ReviewStart { level: level_index }. b) Run 'git diff SAVED_HASH HEAD' to get all changes. c) Collect acceptance criteria from all completed stories in this level. d) Spawn claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format json -p with a prompt including the diff and acceptance criteria asking it to output JSON: { "passed": boolean, "fixes": [{ "title": string, "description": string }] }. e) Parse the JSON response (extract from claude JSON wrapper like run_claude_planner does). f) If passed=true, emit ReviewComplete { level, passed: true, fix_count: 0 } and continue. g) If passed=false, create PrdStory entries for each fix with ids like S{original}-fix{n}, execute them using execute_story (they get committed and pushed), then re-review with a new diff from SAVED_HASH to HEAD. h) Maximum 2 review-fix cycles per level - if after 2 rounds review still fails, log a warning via ReviewLog and emit ReviewComplete with passed=false, then continue to next level. Stream review claude output as ReviewLog events. Skip review if no stories in the level completed successfully.

userStories.title: Update TUI dashboard to display review status
userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/screens/execute_dashboard.rs: 1) In the render_story_list function, when app.review_in_progress is true, add a line after the stories of the current review level showing 'Reviewing Level N...' in cyan with a spinner character (use app.tick_count to cycle through spinner chars like ['⠋','⠙','⠹','⠸','⠼','⠴','⠦','⠧','⠇','⠏']). 2) In the render_logs function, when app.review_in_progress is true and active_ids is empty (no stories actively running), show the review_logs in the log panel instead of the 'Waiting for next story...' message. Use a similar scrolling pattern as story logs. When review_in_progress is false but review_logs is not empty and no stories are active, optionally show review logs as fallback content.

userStories.title: Final build verification - zero warnings
userStories.description: Run cargo build on the entire project and fix any warnings or errors. Check for unused imports, unused variables, dead code warnings, or any other compiler warnings across all modified files (events.rs, app.rs, executor.rs, execute_dashboard.rs). Fix all issues. Ensure the project compiles cleanly with zero warnings. Run cargo build (not just cargo check) to ensure full compilation succeeds.
